channel.title: GeoPulse. Geopolitics in brief
channel.description: Signal-first briefings on geopolitics, edited by Lavkesh Dwivedi.

item.title: Six killed in Israeli strikes on south Lebanon: Health Ministry
item.description: Israeli airstrikes in south Lebanon killed six people, according to the Health Ministry. The strikes targeted projectiles launched from Lebanon, which were intercepted by Israel's military. Hezbollah condemned the strikes as a "blatant violation" of ceasefire understandings.

item.title: One down four to go, says Rice, as Arsenal reignite title charge
item.description: Arsenal's manager, Mikel Arteta, expressed optimism about the team's chances of winning the title. Rice, a midfielder, said "one down, four to go" in a statement, indicating the team's determination to win the championship.

item.title: Orbán steps down from Hungarian parliament after landslide defeat
item.description: Viktor Orbán stepped down from the Hungarian parliament after a landslide defeat. The outgoing prime minister will not take up his seat after leading his party back into opposition. Orbán steps down from Hungarian parliament after landslide defeat.

item.title: China Hints Its 4th Aircraft Carrier Will Be Nuclear-Powered
item.description: China announced plans to build its fourth aircraft carrier, which will be powered by nuclear energy. This move marks a significant development in China's military capabilities, as it was the last major country to adopt aircraft carriers.

item.title: Sooryavanshi fastest to reach 1,000 runs in T20s as teenager smashes 36-ball ton in IPL
item.description: Suryakumar Yadav, an Indian cricketer, became the fastest player to reach 1,000 runs in T20s. He achieved this milestone by scoring a 36-ball century in the Indian Premier League. Sooryavanshi fastest to reach 1,000 runs in T20s as teenager smashes 36-ball ton in IPL.

item.title: Russia pounds Ukraine’s Dnipro for 20 hours straight, killing eight
item.description: Mexican authorities are investigating a car crash that killed two US agents, reportedly CIA employees, after a Mexican-led operation to destroy a drug lab. The agents were not permitted to operate in Mexico.

item.title: Man City fight back to beat Southampton and reach fourth straight FA Cup final
item.description: Manchester City came from behind to defeat Southampton 2-1 in the FA Cup semifinals. The win secured City's fourth consecutive appearance in the FA Cup final, where they will face an opponent to be determined.

item.title: Trump cancels US envoys’ trip after Iran’s Araghchi leaves Pakistan
item.description: Trump cancelled a trip by US envoys to Pakistan after Iran's Araghchi left the country. Trump later suggests that next talks will be over phone, saying 'If they want to talk, all they have to do is call!'.

item.title: 4 Killed In Israeli Airstrikes On Lebanon Amid Fragile Ceasefire
item.description: Israeli airstrikes on Lebanon killed four people, targeting loaded rocket launchers belonging to Hezbollah. The strikes occurred amid a fragile ceasefire, with the Israeli military confirming the action. Israeli military said that it had struck loaded rocket launchers belonging to Hezbollah.

item.title: 'Iran Can Call Us Anytime': Trump After Cancelling Top Officials' Pak Trip
item.description: US President Trump cancelled a trip to Pakistan by top officials, citing confusion within the Iranian leadership. Trump criticised the Iranian government for "infighting" and said they could contact him anytime.

item.title: Trump cancels envoys' Pakistan trip, in blow to hopes for Iran war breakthrough
item.description: US President Trump cancelled a planned visit by his special envoys to Pakistan, citing confusion within the Iranian leadership. The trip cancellation is a blow to hopes for a breakthrough in the Iran war.

item.title: Israel airlifts 240 Bnei Menashe members from Mizoram as India maintains security advisory
item.description: Israel airlifted 240 Bnei Menashe members from Mizoram to Tel Aviv. “Operation Wings of Dawn” aims to take individuals to Tel Aviv as part of religious migration. Israel airlifts 240 Bnei Menashe members from Mizoram as India maintains security advisory.

item.title: Iran FM Abbas Araghchi and Pakistan PM Shehbaz Sharif meet in Pakistan
item.description: Iranian FM Abbas Araghchi met Pakistani PM Shehbaz Sharif in Islamabad to revive US talks. Iranian FM Araghchi met Pakistani PM Sharif in Islamabad as part of efforts to revive direct negotiations with the US.

item.title: Palestinians in West Bank and some in Gaza vote in local elections
item.description: Local elections were held in the occupied West Bank and one Gazan city, with Palestinians voting for new leaders. Hamas and other groups did not participate in the elections, limiting the vote's scope.

item.title: Britain courts Trump with King Charles
item.description: King Charles visited the US to help repair strained US-UK relations. On a state visit to the US, the British monarch is being deployed to help steady increasingly strained US-UK relations.

item.title: 2 Missing, 1 Body, 1 Killer: In US University, Bangladeshi Students' Murder
item.description: One body was found after a murder at a US university involving Bangladeshi students. Abugharbieh had studied at USF between 2021 and 2023. (HCSOSheriff). 2 Missing, 1 Body, 1 Killer: In US University, Bangladeshi Students' Murder.

item.title: Indigenous Speakers Are Booed During Commemorations of Australia’s War Dead
item.description: Indigenous speakers were booed during Anzac Day commemorations in three Australian cities. The disruptions appeared to target a widespread Aboriginal custom acknowledging the land's original inhabitants. The incidents drew attention to ongoing tensions between Indigenous and non-Indigenous Australians.

item.title: The 85-Year-Old Widow Snagged by Trump’s Immigration Crackdown
item.description: Marie-Thérèse Ross-Mahé, an 85-year-old French widow, was deported by US authorities. In her first interview since being deported, Marie-Thérèse Ross-Mahé, the French widow of a former G. I., recounted her experience in ICE detention.

item.title: Paris and Athens exchange mutual defense pledges amid geopolitical turmoil - politico.eu
item.description: Paris and Athens exchanged mutual defense pledges amid geopolitical turmoil. Paris and Athens exchange mutual defense pledges amid geopolitical turmoil politico. eu. Paris and Athens exchange mutual defense pledges amid geopolitical turmoil - politico. eu.

item.title: What counts as the woods? Judge axes Nova Scotia’s ban that defied ‘commonsense definitions’
item.description: A Canadian court overturned Nova Scotia's woods ban. The ruling sided with a hiker who challenged the order.
